title: Vax!
subtitle: Stop a contagion spreading through a social network by vaccinating and quarantining.
learning_goals: Explain how vaccinating well-connected (high-centrality) individuals in a social network reduces overall outbreak size through herd immunity.
Distinguish vaccination (pre-outbreak prevention) from quarantine (mid-outbreak containment) as epidemic interventions.
Predict qualitatively how the structure of a contact network — hubs, clustering, average degree — shapes the trajectory of a contagion.
creator: Ellsworth Campbell, Isaac Bromley, et al.
institution: Salathé Group, Penn State University (original); Digital Epidemiology Lab, EPFL (publishing org)
notes: Vax! was originally created by Ellsworth Campbell as a PhD student in the Salathé Group at Penn State University, with graphic design by Isaac Bromley, and released by the Digital Epidemiology Lab (https://github.com/digitalepidemiologylab/VaxGame) under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 license. The original was a Ruby on Rails application; it was later refactored into a fully static HTML/CSS/JS app by GitHub user rwilliams (https://github.com/cadms/VaxGame), which is the direct upstream of this Galvoro capsule. This Galvoro fork makes only the changes necessary to satisfy the Galvoro Capsule Contribution Spec (removal of Rails artifacts and external link navigation, addition of capsule metadata, restyling of the home screen to surface PLAY (Game) and LEARN (Herd Immunity) as the two primary entry points, and minor CSS adjustments to keep the Herd Immunity module within a single viewport) and does not modify the gameplay, simulation logic, or visual design of the original. The Herd Immunity module, network simulation, scenarios, and tutorial (presented as 'Tutorial' on the home screen, formerly 'Tour') are all carried over verbatim from the cadms/VaxGame upstream. All gameplay credit belongs to the original authors.
